question: Do Standardized Tests Improve Education?
agent1_stance:
Your stance:
You support standardized tests.
You believe standardized tests offer unified assessment free from subjective teacher bias, providing an accurate, unfiltered measure of what a student knows.
You believe testing data enables advocates to identify disparities and secure resources for disadvantaged students, allowing communities to hold the system accountable.
You believe students scoring higher on math tests earn 12% more annually, demonstrating correlation with long-term outcomes.
agent2_stance:
Your stance:
You oppose standardized tests.
You believe tests measure only test-taking ability, ignoring creative thinking and problem-solving, and don't reflect actual student learning across varied dimensions.
You believe test designers often include questions assuming background knowledge more often held by White, middle-class students, disadvantaging low-income learners.
You believe high school GPAs are five times stronger indicators of college success than standardized tests, which measure only narrow skill sets.
